_about: Bewiched EOS Scorecard data. Written by run_weekly.py pull_eos_scorecard(); rendered by gen_eos_scorecard.py. Live = BigQuery; derived = other feeds; manual = inputs sheet.

weekly.name: YoY Sales Growth
weekly.note: Last completed week vs same week last year (LFL); reuses the weekly sales pull (lw26/lw25).

weekly.name: YoY Transactional Growth
weekly.note: Last completed week transactions vs same week last year (LFL); reuses tx26/tx25.

weekly.name: Google Health
weekly.detail: Coverage 52% (11/21) · 29 reviews vs target 50 · 5.0★ avg — last week
weekly.note: Coverage × volume × rating: (stores with ≥1 review ÷ 21) × [0.5·min(reviews÷50,1) + 0.5·min(rating÷4.6,1)] × 100. No-review stores pull it down. Last completed week. Green ≥ 70.

weekly.name: Rate My Shift Health
weekly.note: Blend: avg of submissions÷70 and avgScore÷4.6, each capped 100%. Last completed week.

weekly.name: Brew Crew Kudos Participation
weekly.detail: 50 of 188 employees gave kudos last week (BCKH)
weekly.note: Distinct employees who contributed to Brew Crew Kudos (BCKH tab, F1 workbook) in the LAST COMPLETED WEEK, matched by email to the Employee List, ÷ total employees. Awaiting if no entries that week.

weekly.name: Social Media Engagement
weekly.note: Metric and target not yet defined.

weekly.name: SPH Labour (incl holiday pay)
weekly.detail: £163201 sales ÷ 2904 hours used (last week, 17 stores reporting)
weekly.note: Sales per labour hour incl holiday pay. Last completed week; provisional on Sunday, finalised Monday once planner hours post.

weekly.name: Bench
weekly.detail: Net SM on the bench: 4 live Store Manager vacancies now (actual -4) vs a +3 surplus target — 7 off plan.
weekly.note: MAIN KPI = NET Store Managers on the bench. Actual = -(Store Manager vacancies) from the HRP 'HRP & Bench' roster (the red 'Gap / no SM' stores); target = +3 (a surplus of ready SM cover); miss = target - actual. The star map, management-team table and Bench-ready / Thin / Capability-gap cards below use the hierarchy-gap rule, unchanged and byte-identical to the Company Dashboard bench tab.

weekly.name: F1 Score
weekly.detail: Last week's race result, estate avg (21 stores) — lower is better
weekly.note: Metric = AVERAGE RACE TOTAL SCORE, and LOWER IS BETTER on this scale. Target ≤220 — green when the average score is at or below 220, red when above. (Estate ~282 now, so RED.) The old '75' higher-is-better target is retired.

weekly.name: Brand & Remote Assessment
weekly.detail: Blended brand audit + remote assessment, last completed week (estate)
weekly.note: Last completed week's 50/50 blend of brand audit and remote assessment (whichever is present that week). Both are periodic — the QTD tile is the reliable one.

weekly.name: Food GP%
weekly.detail: Cost-of-Sales estate Gross Profit% (col Q), latest week ending 2026-07-20
weekly.note: Estate Gross Profit% from the Cost-of-Sales sheet (col Q, sales-weighted across stores).

weekly.name: Net Profit After Tax (projected)
weekly.note: Projected (GP + labour flex off the May 2026 P&L). NPAT% = baseline 7.9% + (estate GP% − May 2026 baseline) − (labour% − baseline). Baseline: product GP 66.1%, labour 33.9%, admin 24.3% (held), avg labour £19.53/hr. GP movement = estate Gross Profit% from the CoS sheet (col Q, sales-weighted; May 2026 baseline 70.11%, latest week 70.85%, QTD 70.53%). Labour flexes via planner actual CPH £56.2 ÷ baseline £57.7 (avg £19.53/hr ÷ live CPH). Weekly CPH = this week's planner CPH; QTD CPH £56.6 is hours-weighted from weekly_history.csv (3 weeks so far).

weekly.name: New Starter Health
weekly.detail: Onboarding compliance — 1 of 29 new starters clear of every due step (first 90 days)
weekly.note: Youda onboarding: share of first-90-day starters compliant on every due step. From new_starter.json (committed; refreshed from Youda on its own schedule). Target 90%.

quarterly.name: YoY Sales Growth
quarterly.detail: LFL QTD sales vs same period last year (18 like-for-like stores)

quarterly.name: YoY Transactional Growth
quarterly.detail: LFL QTD transactions vs last year (18 like-for-like stores)

quarterly.name: Google Health
quarterly.note: Coverage × volume × rating: (QTD stores with a review ÷ 21) × [0.5·min(reviews÷(50×3 wks),1) + 0.5·min(rating÷4.6,1)] × 100. Green ≥ 70.

quarterly.name: Rate My Shift Health
quarterly.note: Blend: avg of QTD submissions÷(70×3 wks) and avg score÷4.6, each capped 100%.

quarterly.name: Brew Crew Kudos Participation
quarterly.detail: 68 of 188 employees gave kudos this quarter (BCKH)
quarterly.note: Distinct employees who contributed to Brew Crew Kudos (BCKH tab) QUARTER-TO-DATE, matched by email to the Employee List, ÷ total employees.

quarterly.name: Social Media Engagement
quarterly.note: Metric and target not yet defined.

quarterly.name: SPH Labour (incl holiday pay)
quarterly.detail: QTD £/hr, hours-weighted from weekly_history (3 weeks so far)
quarterly.note: QTD sales per labour hour, hours-weighted across the weekly_history.csv rows since quarter start. Thin until several weeks accumulate (falls back to the current week).

quarterly.name: Bench
quarterly.detail: Net SM on the bench: 4 live Store Manager vacancies now (actual -4) vs a +3 surplus target — 7 off plan.
quarterly.note: Point-in-time net Store Managers on the bench (same as weekly): actual = -(SM vacancies), target = +3, miss = target - actual. Detail below is byte-identical to the Company Dashboard bench tab (hierarchy-gap rule).

quarterly.name: F1 Score
quarterly.detail: QTD race 'Total Score', estate avg (21 stores) — lower is better
quarterly.note: Metric = AVERAGE RACE TOTAL SCORE, and LOWER IS BETTER on this scale. Target ≤220 — green when the average score is at or below 220, red when above. (Estate ~282 now, so RED.) The old '75' higher-is-better target is retired.

quarterly.name: Brand & Remote Assessment
quarterly.detail: Estate 50/50 blend of brand audit + remote assessment (QTD), out of 5
quarterly.note: Each store blends its QTD brand audit (/5) and remote assessment (out of 100, divided by 20) 50/50; a store with only one uses that one. Estate = average of per-store blends. Sources: Brand Audit sheet + 'Remote Assessment Data' tab (Richard Wagg).

quarterly.name: Food GP%
quarterly.detail: Estate Gross Profit% (col Q) from Cost of Sales, quarter-to-date (sales-weighted)
quarterly.note: Estate Gross Profit% from the Cost-of-Sales sheet (col Q, sales-weighted), quarter-to-date.

quarterly.name: Net Profit After Tax (projected)
quarterly.note: Projected (GP + labour flex off the May 2026 P&L). NPAT% = baseline 7.9% + (estate GP% − May 2026 baseline) − (labour% − baseline). Baseline: product GP 66.1%, labour 33.9%, admin 24.3% (held), avg labour £19.53/hr. GP movement = estate Gross Profit% from the CoS sheet (col Q, sales-weighted; May 2026 baseline 70.11%, latest week 70.85%, QTD 70.53%). Labour flexes via planner actual CPH £56.2 ÷ baseline £57.7 (avg £19.53/hr ÷ live CPH). Weekly CPH = this week's planner CPH; QTD CPH £56.6 is hours-weighted from weekly_history.csv (3 weeks so far).

quarterly.name: New Starter Health
quarterly.detail: Onboarding compliance — 1 of 29 new starters clear of every due step (first 90 days)
quarterly.note: Youda onboarding: share of first-90-day starters compliant on every due step. From new_starter.json (committed; refreshed from Youda on its own schedule). Target 90%.

weekly.basis: Last completed week vs 2025, like-for-like (per store)
Last completed week transactions vs 2025, like-for-like (per store)
Cost-of-Sales latest week, per store (col Q Gross Profit%)

qtd.basis: Cost-of-Sales quarter-to-date, per store (col Q, sales-weighted)

weekly.basis: Last completed week sales ÷ planner hours used, vs each store's own £/hr target (Store-Targets sheet)

qtd.basis: Per-store SPH vs each store's own target — QTD labour hours aren't separately sourced, so this mirrors the last completed week

weekly.basis: Named bench successors per store (point-in-time — same each period)

qtd.basis: Named bench successors per store (point-in-time — same each period)

weekly.basis: Last completed week race Total Score (per store)

qtd.basis: QTD average race Total Score (per store)
QTD blend: review volume (÷120) & rating (÷4.6), per store
QTD blend: submission volume (÷210) & score (÷4.6), per store
QTD 50/50 blend of brand audit + remote assessment, out of 5 (per store)

weekly.basis: Distinct employees at the store who gave kudos last week ÷ store headcount (per store, vs 50% plan)

qtd.basis: Distinct employees at the store who gave kudos this quarter ÷ store headcount (per store, vs 50% plan)

new_starter._schema_note: per_starter[].steps = {stepLabel: done|overdue|due|na}. Cohort = genuine active first-90-day starters with a Youda onboarding record. Statuses pulled live from Youda getJourneys.

weekly.basis: Last completed week sales ÷ transactions (per store)
Food or Bakery guest-checks ÷ transactions, last completed week (per store)

qtd.basis: Food or Bakery guest-checks ÷ transactions, quarter-to-date (per store)

flags: Status is strictly binary: GREEN when actual ≥ plan, RED when below — no near-target band. Bench is green when ≥ 3.
Google Health & Rate My Shift Health blend divisors (40 reviews / 4.6★ ; 70 submissions / 4.6★) are default assumptions — adjust if you prefer different volume targets.
Plans (Matt's stated defaults): SPH Labour 55, Brew Crew Kudos 50%, Bench 3, NPAT 18%, Food GP% 71%. YoY Sales 12% / Transactions 5% on both tabs.

F1 Score = AVERAGE RACE TOTAL SCORE (Matt confirmed), live from the F1 sheet (ID 1YFqpR9_ftlQEbfwc5ZVMjtS5tFO0j-7ccwB8rwG56wQ) — weekly = last week's race, quarterly = QTD avg. LOWER IS BETTER on this scale: target ≤220, green at or below 220 and red above (estate ~282 now, so RED). The old '75' higher-is-better target is retired.

SYMMETRIC: both tabs now carry the SAME 13 KPIs — Weekly measured on the last completed week, Quarterly the identical 13 measured QTD (since quarter start). Where a measure has no natural weekly/QTD split it shows the same figure on both tabs (see below).

Same figure on both tabs (by nature): NPAT (latest-month P&L projection — no weekly actual), SPH Labour (a £/hr rate — QTD labour hours not separately sourced), Bench (point-in-time headcount), Food GP% (weekly CoS, a week in arrears). Brand Audit weekly shows 'awaiting' in weeks with no audits; the QTD tile is the reliable one.

Still need definitions/sources: New Starter Health and Social Media Engagement are greyed TBC placeholders on BOTH tabs until Matt defines the metric + source. NPAT needs the P&L sheet shared with the service account to go beyond the May snapshot.

Net Profit After Tax is a PROJECTION model: May P&L baseline (product GP 66.1%, labour 33.85%, admin 24.33%, NPAT 7.9%, avg labour £19.53/hr) flexed by live GP movement (CoS blended GP, commercial-store proxy) and live SPH labour. This period GP/SPH match baseline so both tiles read ~7.9%; they flex as GP/SPH move. Share the P&L sheet with the SA so the baseline auto-refreshes monthly.
Food GP% uses the Cost of Sales estate GP% as a proxy until a company food-specific GP source exists.
Social Media Engagement and New Starter Health are greyed TBC placeholders pending metric + target definitions.

Each metric now shows an accountable OWNER (EOS-style): YoY Sales/Transactions & Food GP% = Rich; Google Health, Social Media & SPH Labour = Jon; Rate My Shift, Brew Crew Kudos, Bench & New Starter = Kel; F1 & Brand Audit = Claire. Net Profit After Tax is UNASSIGNED (shown as —) — Matt to confirm the owner (likely Matt/MD). Owners are a config block in gen_eos_scorecard.py.

NEW third tab 'Quarterly Scorecard' — classic EOS grid: the 13 metrics as rows (owner + plan) × each week of the quarter as columns, traffic-lit binary. Back-filled from source: BigQuery (sales, YoY sales/tx per week), COS master (estate GP + NPAT projection per week), F1 sheet (race score), Shift Ratings (RMS), Reviews (Google Health), BCKH (Kudos). SPH (no historical hours), Bench (point-in-time) and Brand Audit (sparse) fill going forward; Social Media & New Starter stay TBC. The grid reads weekly_history.csv.

QTD CPH/SPH and the labour side of QTD NPAT now read from the committed weekly_history.csv (one row per week-ending, upserted each run — re-runs update, no dupes). Thin until several weeks accumulate; until then QTD ≈ the current week. YoY (BigQuery), Kudos QTD (BCKH), GP QTD (COS master) keep their own source-of-truth and are also logged to history.

Manual inputs sheet 'Bewiched EOS Scorecard Inputs' (ID 1HimYAjZg4zlMQG91-KUefkeYMPvrU4ddVuO2IuERTqg) must be shared (Viewer) with dashboards-bot@bewiched-coffee-368116.iam.gserviceaccount.com for the automated run to read it.

Manual inputs sheet not readable (<HttpError 403 when requesting https://sheets.googleapis.com/v4/spreadsheets/1HimYAjZg4zlM) — share it (Viewer) with the service account dashboards-bot@bewiched-coffee-368116.iam.gserviceaccount.com. Manual metrics shown as awaiting.

Brew Crew Kudos per-store: 3 QTD contributor(s) matched no employee (head-office/ex-staff — excluded, as in the company calc); 5 matched contributor(s) have no store Location (e.g. Head Office — excluded from the per-store split but still in the company headcount). 21 stores resolved from the Employee List Location column.

Net Profit After Tax: P&L sheet not readable by the service account (<HttpError 403 when requesting https://sheets.googleapis.com) — share 'May 2026 P&L' (ID 1RTsnnz5F9XIdkg4j8m8MiuKqeAvZaAWcndbFifNNLhM, Viewer) with dashboards-bot@bewiched-coffee-368116.iam.gserviceaccount.com. Using FROZEN May baseline constants.
